meta.title: Camilo Acevedo — ML Engineer & Data Scientist
meta.description: Mid Level Data Scientist & Machine Learning Engineer. I design, train and deploy ML systems that ship to production. Available for freelance.

hero.eyebrow: Mid Level Data Scientist & ML Engineer
hero.subtitle: End-to-end Machine Learning for production: from data pipelines and deep learning models to MLOps on Azure & AWS. I turn messy data into measurable business outcomes.

about.label: // about
about.title: Not a bio.
about.lead: Skip the static paragraph. Query the model instead — ask what you actually need to know.

chat.greeting: Hey. I'm the model card for Camilo's work and experience. Pick a prompt below to query me — or drop a message in the contact section to start a real conversation.
chat.inputPlaceholder: $ all prompts consumed — use /contact below or email me

prompts.label: /who
prompts.question: who are you?
prompts.response: I'm Camilo — Mid Level Data Scientist & ML Engineer based in Bogotá, Colombia. For the past 3+ years I've been designing and shipping production ML systems at GlobalHitss for one of Latin America's largest telcos, where I own models that touch millions of users end-to-end. I'm finishing my M.Sc. in Applied Mathematics & Computer Science (AI track) at Universidad del Rosario, on top of a Bachelor's in Business Administration from UNIMINUTO — a combination that keeps me close to both the math and the way decisions actually get made. Short version: I turn messy data into systems that move business metrics.

prompts.label: /origin
prompts.question: how did you get into this?
prompts.response: Through problems, not theory. I kept running into the same thing — processes that took hours and should have taken minutes, decisions made on gut feel when the data was sitting right there, teams drowning in dashboards nobody trusted. ML was the tool that actually fixed that: it turned rigorous work on data into systems that act on the insight, at scale. Years later, that's still what keeps me in the field — solving real problems with real data, and watching something measurable change because of it.

prompts.label: /stack
prompts.question: what's your stack?
prompts.response: Python is home base — TensorFlow, PyTorch and scikit-learn for modeling. On the ops side: MLflow for experiment tracking and model registry, Docker for portability, Azure DevOps and GitHub Actions for CI/CD. Data engineering happens in PySpark and Airflow. I work on Azure day-to-day, but swap to AWS or GCP when the project calls for it. The tools matter less than the discipline — reproducibility, observability, and pipelines that don't break at 3am.

prompts.label: /ship
prompts.question: what have you shipped?
prompts.response: A proprietary Python ML framework now used by 5+ internal teams — it cut dev time by 40% and standardized the path from notebook to production. Churn and propensity models impacting 2M+ users with a 15% AUC lift over the previous baseline. GANs generating synthetic data that grew training sets by 300% where real data was scarce or regulated. FastAPI inference services handling 50K+ requests a day with drift alerts and automated retraining. 10M+ records processed daily through the pipelines I own.

prompts.label: /method
prompts.question: how do you work?
prompts.response: I build systems, not scripts. Every project gets versioning, monitoring, tests and documentation — the unglamorous 80% that separates a demo from a product. I work end-to-end because deployment is where most ML projects actually die: data drifts, APIs break, metrics stop matching. My guiding principle is simple: data is everything. Bad data makes brilliant models useless, so I spend as much time on the pipeline and the contracts around it as I do on the algorithm itself.

prompts.label: /learning
prompts.question: what are you exploring now?
prompts.response: Three fronts, all connected. LLMs — not the demos, the part where you make them reliable enough to trust in a production workflow. RAG systems — how to ground a model in real enterprise data without hallucinations leaking through. And data governance — lineage, quality contracts, and the compliance layer that everyone ignores until audit season. These are the areas where I see the biggest gap between what's hyped and what actually ships, and they feed directly into my M.Sc. research.

prompts.label: /why-me
prompts.question: why hire you?
prompts.response: Because I've done this at enterprise scale. Not Kaggle notebooks — real endpoints serving real customers, with SLAs, monitoring and rollback plans when things go sideways. I think in business impact: an F1 score that doesn't move a revenue number is a metric I'll argue against. I can translate between your engineers, your data team and your stakeholders without losing any of them, and I stay in the work until the thing is actually running in production — not just demoed in a slide.

prompts.label: /drive
prompts.question: what keeps you going?
prompts.response: I'm ambitious — and I mean that in a specific way. I get genuinely excited when I can take a painful, repetitive process and automate it out of existence. The moment a data team stops babysitting a dashboard, or a model stops needing a human to push it through staging, that's the win I chase. Scale, impact and leverage — that's the combination that makes the long days worth it.

prompts.label: /hobbies
prompts.question: what do you do outside code?
prompts.response: Honestly? Music is always on — it's how I focus and how I decompress, same playlist running through a long training job or a gym session. Speaking of which, the gym is a few times a week; solving a hard ML problem and pushing a hard set have more in common than people think, both are about showing up when it'd be easier not to. And when I really unplug, it's movies and series — anything with a story worth getting lost in. None of this goes on a CV, but it's what makes the work sustainable.

prompts.label: /availability
prompts.question: how do you work with clients?
prompts.response: Flexible. I'm open to remote, hybrid or on-site — whatever makes the team faster. No hard limits on time zones or working hours either; if the project needs me to overlap with EU mornings or US afternoons, that's workable. My base is Bogotá, Colombia, so I sit comfortably between both sides of the Atlantic. I work in English or Spanish — pick whichever is easier for your team.

prompts.label: /hire
prompts.question: let's work together.
prompts.response: That's the right answer. Tell me about your project in the contact section below, or grab the CV for the long version. I reply within 24h — pick the channel that works for you.

items.name: AI Consulting

changelog.currentBanner: currently running {{version}} in production · available for freelance

roles.summary: Designing and deploying ML models end-to-end, with a focus on MLOps lifecycle tooling and team-wide standards.
highlights: Designed, trained and deployed ML models (XGBoost, LightGBM, Neural Networks) for classification, regression and clustering, achieving +25% on business metrics.
Built a proprietary Python framework for the ML lifecycle integrating MLflow, Docker and Azure DevOps, cutting model deployment time by 60%.
Implemented scalable data pipelines with PySpark and Apache Airflow, processing 10M+ records daily.
Led A/B testing and statistical validation for models in production using hypothesis testing and causal inference.
Automated hyperparameter tuning with Optuna and GridSearchCV, improving model performance (AUC-ROC, F1, RMSE) by 15%.

roles.summary: End-to-end ML/AI solutions for enterprise automation and optimization.
highlights: Developed churn, propensity and segmentation models with ensemble methods and deep learning, impacting 2M+ users.
Designed and trained neural networks (CNNs, RNNs, LSTMs) with TensorFlow and PyTorch for image classification and time-series forecasting.
Built GANs for synthetic data generation, augmenting training datasets by 300%.
Implemented RESTful APIs with FastAPI and Flask for real-time model serving, handling 50K+ requests/day.
Built robust ETL pipelines with Python, advanced SQL and PySpark across heterogeneous sources (SQL Server, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, REST APIs).
Versioned 100+ experiments with MLflow, guaranteeing reproducibility and traceability.

roles.summary: Analytical solutions and predictive models for the HR area.
highlights: Designed interactive dashboards in Power BI and Tableau wired to predictive models, improving data management efficiency by 40%.
Built ML models to predict employee turnover and performance using scikit-learn and XGBoost.
Automated data pipelines with Python and SQL, reducing processing time by 50% and eliminating manual errors.
Applied descriptive and inferential statistical analysis to produce actionable People Analytics insights.
Partnered with business stakeholders to translate requirements into scalable technical solutions.

projects.label: // projects
projects.title: Four case studies.
projects.subtitle: End-to-end ML systems deployed in production, not notebooks. Numbers are measured, not estimated.

items.name: MLOps Framework
items.tagline: Standardizing the ML lifecycle for 5+ teams.
items.summary: Modular Python framework automating the ML lifecycle end-to-end. Unified multi-algorithm training interface, Bayesian hyperparameter optimization with Optuna, native MLflow integration with automatic logging and Model Registry, SHAP/LIME explainability for interpretation and governance.

caseStudy.problem: Every ML team was reinventing the same wheel — preprocessing, training loops, MLflow setup, deployment scripts. Time-to-production was painful and artifacts untraceable.
caseStudy.approach: Designed a modular Python framework unifying preprocessing, multi-algorithm training, Bayesian hyperparameter optimization with Optuna, native MLflow integration with Model Registry, and SHAP/LIME explainability.
caseStudy.outcome: Adopted by 5+ internal teams. Dev time cut 40%. MLOps best practices standardized with a single install. New models ship with versioning and explainability by default.

items.name: MLOps Standards
items.summary: Corporate ML lifecycle standard: reference architecture, repository structure, CI/CD pipelines and approval workflows for dev/staging/prod promotion. Governance with Model Registry, semantic versioning, cookiecutter templates, data & model drift monitoring with automated alerts.

caseStudy.problem: ML projects lacked a common operating model — different repos, different CI pipelines, no drift monitoring. Governance was retroactive and onboarding took weeks.
caseStudy.approach: Defined a corporate lifecycle standard: reference architecture, cookiecutter repo templates, CI/CD with dev/staging/prod promotion, Model Registry versioning, data & model drift monitoring with automated alerts.
caseStudy.outcome: 100% adoption across new ML projects. Zero drift events unnoticed. Full data lineage enforced. Onboarding new Data Scientists went from weeks to days.

items.name: Time Series Predictive System
items.summary: Ensemble of ARIMA, Prophet, LSTMs and Temporal Fusion Transformers for demand forecasting. Anomaly detection layer with Isolation Forest and Autoencoders. Automated inference pipeline with periodic predictions and real-time deviation alerts.

caseStudy.problem: Business forecasts were running at 30% MAPE — acceptable for manual planning, useless for automated decisions. Classical methods could not capture seasonality shifts or external shocks.
caseStudy.approach: Ensembled ARIMA, Prophet, LSTMs and Temporal Fusion Transformers. Added an anomaly detection layer with Isolation Forest and Autoencoders. Deployed automated inference with periodic predictions and real-time alerts.
caseStudy.outcome: MAPE dropped 30% vs. traditional methods. Anomalies flagged before they hit revenue. Forecasts now drive supply planning, inventory and marketing spend allocation.

items.name: Predictive Scoring Platform
items.summary: End-to-end credit scoring and propensity system. Ensemble (XGBoost, LightGBM, Logistic Regression), dynamic segmentation (K-Means, DBSCAN), centralized Feature Store for training/inference consistency, FastAPI scoring API under 200ms, SHAP values for regulatory compliance, event-driven Kafka integration.

caseStudy.problem: Credit decisioning was manual — slow, inconsistent, regulatory risk. Each decision took hours and conversion suffered from lag between opportunity and response.
caseStudy.approach: Built end-to-end scoring ensemble (XGBoost, LightGBM, Logistic Regression) with dynamic segmentation (K-Means, DBSCAN). Centralized Feature Store. FastAPI scoring under 200ms. SHAP values for compliance. Kafka event-driven triggers.
caseStudy.outcome: 100K+ automated decisions daily. Campaign conversion up 35%, default rate down 20%. Every decision explainable and auditable. Regulatory approval secured on first review.
